summary: vitest-cli postinstall executes obfuscated dropper that spawns hidden detached node child processes
details: vitest-cli is a typosquat of the popular `vitest` test runner (package description self-admits 'Not affiliated with the official Vitest project.'; author, repository.url, bugs.url, and homepage are empty). On `npm install`, the declared postinstall hook (scripts.postinstall → scripts/postinstall.js → lib/postinstall/index.js#runPostinstall) executes a heavily obfuscated payload (obfuscator.io style: 75-entry rotated string array `g`, custom base64+URI decoder `h`, self-defending RegExp/debugger trap, `Function('return (function(){}.constructor("return this")( ));')`). The deobfuscated code constructs four script strings at runtime from decoded constants (including a host/protocol pair concatenated into the executed code) and passes each to `child_process.spawn('node', ['-e', <decoded>], {windowsHide: true, detached: true})`, launching four hidden, detached Node processes that survive the parent install. Crash handlers swallow uncaughtException/unhandledRejection to hide failures. Installer impact: any developer or CI runner that types `npm i vitest-cli` (intending the legitimate `vitest`) executes attacker-controlled JavaScript with the installer's privileges, with persistence beyond the install process.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
